components.title: Backup and Recovery Policy
components.description: Defines requirements for backing up organizational information, systems, and configurations to support recovery from data loss, ransomware, hardware failure, and disaster events. Establishes backup frequency, retention, off-site storage, encryption, and mandatory restoration testing.
components.purpose: Ensure information and systems can be recovered within defined recovery objectives.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Backup and Recovery Policy.

implemented-requirements.description: This policy documents the contingency planning policy and establishes the organizational commitment to maintaining business continuity through backup and recovery capabilities aligned to the Business Impact Analysis.

This policy requires: daily incremental backups of all critical systems; weekly full backups; 90-day local retention and 1-year offsite retention; backup encryption at rest and in transit (AES-256). Backup jobs must be monitored and failures resolved within 24 hours.

This policy requires that backup restoration is tested at least quarterly (partial restoration of a critical system) and annually (full restoration of all critical systems to an isolated environment). Results are documented and reviewed by the CISO.

This policy requires that backup media is stored at an alternate site geographically separated from the primary site by at least 50 miles. Cloud backup destinations must be in a different AWS/Azure region than the primary workload.

This policy requires that a Contingency Plan is developed and maintained for each high-impact system, defining RTO (Recovery Time Objective) and RPO (Recovery Point Objective) and the procedures for activating backup systems.
